skill.name: literature-extensive-read
skill.description: Rapidly skim and summarize academic papers (default:PDF-to-Markdown full text with `## Page XX` pagination and image references) and output a structured extensive-reading summary in Markdown when you need to quickly understand research questions, methods, key results, conclusions, and decide whether intensive reading is worthwhile.
skill.summary: Read academic papers quickly and produce structured summaries for literature triage.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 41 static findings are false positives. The 14 weak cryptographic algorithm findings reference no actual crypto operations. The 25 shell backtick execution findings are Markdown inline code formatting, not Ruby execution. The 6 system reconnaissance findings are test case labels in an audit JSON file. The 2 hardcoded URL findings point to a legitimate GitHub source repository. This skill contains only Markdown documentation files with no executable code, no network exfiltration, and no command injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Markdown inline code detected as shell execution
low_findings.description: 25 instances of Markdown backtick formatting (e.g., `## Page XX`, `outputs/`, `pdf-extract`) were flagged as Ruby shell backtick execution. All are standard Markdown inline code formatting, not executable commands. No actual shell execution is present.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positive
low_findings.description: 14 instances flagged as weak crypto. No cryptographic algorithms appear in any file. The files contain only Markdown documentation and JSON audit data. The scanner likely misidentified non-crypto text patterns.

low_findings.title: Legitimate GitHub source URL flagged as network threat
low_findings.description: The URL https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills is a legitimate source attribution link in the skill documentation. It links to the author's public GitHub repository for this skill. No data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Audit test case labels flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: 6 instances of text labels in literature-extensive-read_audit_result_v1.json were flagged as system reconnaissance. These are test case descriptions and notes from a previous skill evaluation, not actual reconnaissance commands.

content.user_title: Read and Summarize Academic Papers Faster
content.value_statement: Reading many academic papers is time-consuming. This skill generates structured summaries so you can quickly decide which papers need a closer look.
actual_capabilities: Extract research questions, methods, results, and conclusions from academic papers
Generate structured Markdown summaries using a predefined output template
Prioritize reading by scanning title, abstract, and conclusion first
Provide intensive reading recommendations with supporting reasons
Process PDF-to-Markdown converted academic texts with page markers
Identify missing information and mark it as Not specified without guessing
limitations: Cannot open or read PDF files directly without an external converter
Cannot interpret figures, tables, or images beyond what the text describes
Does not verify the factual accuracy or correctness of paper claims
May produce incomplete summaries on papers with non-standard structure

use_cases.title: Screen Papers for a Research Project
use_cases.description: Quickly review dozens of academic papers to find relevant studies for your research.

use_cases.title: Triage Reading for Busy Professionals
use_cases.description: Decide which papers to read in full based on structured summaries and recommendations.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Systematic Literature Review
use_cases.description: Create consistent structured summaries across many papers to compare findings and methods.
use_cases.target_user: Systematic review teams and evidence analysts

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paper Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the attached academic paper text and produce a structured summary. Use the template from assets/rapid_summary_template.md. Include the research question, methodology overview, main results, and conclusion.
prompt_templates.scenario: First time using the skill to summarize a single paper

prompt_templates.title: Summary with Intensive Reading Recommendation
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this paper and produce a summary. Evaluate whether the paper is worth reading in full. Consider the clarity of evidence and relevance to my research on [your topic]. Provide a yes or no recommendation with reasons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deciding whether to invest time in a full paper reading

prompt_templates.title: Comparison-Focused Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this paper and create a summary. Focus on how the methods and results compare to prior work in [specific field]. Highlight what is new or different in this paper.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding how a paper advances the field

prompt_templates.title: Methods and Reproducibility Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this paper and summarize it. Give extra attention to the methods section. List the dataset, experimental setup, models used, evaluation metrics, and key parameters. Note any missing details that would affect reproducibility.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating whether a paper can be reproduced or applied

output_examples.input: Read this paper on deep learning for medical image analysis and create a summary.
output_examples.output:
# Literature Survey: Deep Learning for Medical Image Analysis

## Basic Information
- Title: Deep Learning for Medical Image Analysis: A Survey
- Author(s): Zhang et al.
- Year/Journal: 2023, Nature Reviews
- Research Field: Medical Imaging

## Research Question
How can deep learning models improve diagnosis accuracy in medical imaging?

## Methodology Overview
The authors reviewed 150 studies published between 2018 and 2023. They compared CNN and transformer-based models across CT, MRI, and X-ray modalities.

## Main Results
CNN models achieved 92% average accuracy for classification tasks. Transformer models performed better on segmentation tasks with 88% mean IoU.

## Conclusion and Significance
Deep learning shows strong potential for clinical decision support but requires more diverse training data.

## Intensive Reading Judgment
- Intensive Reading Required: Yes
- Reason: The paper provides a comprehensive benchmark across multiple imaging modalities.

output_examples.input: Review this paper on climate change models and tell me if I should read it fully.
output_examples.output:
# Literature Survey: Advances in Climate Change Modeling

## Basic Information
- Title: Advances in Climate Change Modeling: 2020-2023
- Author(s): Kumar and Patel
- Year/Journal: 2023, Journal of Climate
- Research Field: Climate Science

## Research Question
What improvements have been made in regional climate prediction accuracy?

## Methodology Overview
The authors evaluated 12 climate models using historical temperature and precipitation data from 2000 to 2020.

## Main Results
Regional prediction accuracy improved by 15% compared to previous models. Uncertainty in long-term projections remains high.

## Conclusion and Significance
The improvements are meaningful for short-term regional planning but limited for long-term projections.

## Intensive Reading Judgment
- Intensive Reading Required: No
- Reason: The findings are incremental and the uncertainty analysis is not fully explained.

best_practices: Read title, abstract, and conclusion first before scanning methods and results for deeper understanding.
Mark any missing information as Not specified instead of adding guesses or inferred content.
Use the same output template for every paper to enable easy comparison across your literature collection.
anti_patterns: Do not add interpretation that goes beyond what the paper explicitly states in its text.
Do not skip the methods section if you need to understand how results were produced and verified.
Do not treat the summary as a complete substitute for reading important papers in full.

faq.question: Can this skill read PDF files directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill needs text converted from PDF to Markdown first. Use a tool like pdf-extract.

faq.question: Does the skill check if the paper is scientifically accurate?
faq.answer: No. The skill only extracts and organizes what the paper says. It does not verify claims.

faq.question: Can I compare multiple papers with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The consistent output template makes it easy to compare summaries side by side.

faq.question: What happens if the paper has no methods section?
faq.answer: The skill marks missing fields as Not specified in the output summary.

faq.question: Does this skill work with papers in any language?
faq.answer: The default output language is Chinese. You can request output in other languages.

faq.question: Where are the summary files saved?
faq.answer: Summaries are saved as UTF-8 Markdown files in the outputs folder.
